features.summary: Nike delivered exceptional Q4 results with revenue growing 96% to over $12 billion, driven by strong wholesale shipments and store reopenings. Full-year revenue grew 19% with digital business more than doubling to over $9 billion since pre-pandemic levels. The company exceeded 300 million Nike members and achieved 15.5% EBIT margin. Nike Direct now approaches 40% of total brand revenue, with digital representing 21% of total brand revenue, reaching milestones several years ahead of prior plans.
features.management_outlook: Management provided ambitious long-term guidance through fiscal 2025, expecting high single-digit to low double-digit revenue growth on average, reaching over $50 billion in fiscal 2022. They anticipate Nike Direct will represent approximately 60% of business by fiscal 2025, with combined owned and partnered digital reaching 50% mix. Gross margins are expected to reach high 40s by fiscal 2025, with EBIT margins reaching high teens and earnings per share growth of mid-to-high teens annually.
features.risks: Supply chain disruptions and higher logistics costs are expected to persist throughout much of fiscal 2022, with longer end-to-end lead times continuing. Greater China faces ongoing marketplace dynamics that impacted Q4 performance, though sequential improvement is being seen. The company expects non-linear quarterly growth patterns as they normalize post-pandemic operations and continue reshaping their marketplace strategy.
features.key_drivers: Digital transformation remains the primary growth driver, with the Nike app representing 40% of digital business and plans to launch in 10 additional countries in fiscal 2022. Key growth opportunities include women's business (grew 22% full-year), Jordan Brand (reached nearly $5 billion, up 31%), and international expansion. The return of sports and permanent shifts in consumer behavior toward digital and health/wellness create structural tailwinds for continued growth.
